topic: Bermuda Triangle / East Japan Geometric Symmetry
our_argument_summary: Our review verdicts this as 'Refuted by Data' — NOAA states no evidence of anomalous disappearances, Lloyd's does not charge premium rates, USCG found no unusual causes. We now also address Part (2): the dome conflates geographic folklore with geophysics, notes the Bermuda region is not a geomagnetic anomaly (SAA is off Brazil), the Devil's Sea has no distinctive geomagnetic signature, and the claimed 180° symmetry doesn't correspond to IGRF or CHAOS-7 models.

relevant_files: None — WIN-028 is absent from the dome's codebase
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No calculation pipeline exists. WIN-028 appears only as a one-line title ('Bermuda Triangle / East Japan Geometric Symmetry CONFIRMED') in the wins registry. No script computes agonic line positions, geomagnetic symmetry, or disappearance rates. The monitor.py 39-domain engine does not include any domain for this WIN.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Pure static assertion — no code validates the symmetry claim or the disappearance correlation. No geomagnetic declination calculation, no agonic line tracking, no disappearance data anywhere in the codebase.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None — no validation performed at all
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None — our review does not make specific claims about dome code for this WIN, which is appropriate.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: WIN-028 does not appear in any monitoring domain. The dome site lists it only as a one-line confirmed WIN with no monitoring, validation, or data fetch code. Searched all raw-text files — no monitor.py or pull_data.py exist in the extracted content, and no automation references WIN-028.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: The Bermuda Triangle/Devil's Sea 'disappearance zone' concept is paranormal folklore, not standard physics. The agonic line concept is real geophysics but the dome's claimed 180° symmetry correlation with disappearance zones is not a standard physics claim being relabeled — it's a novel (and incorrect) association.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: The Bermuda Triangle legend dates to the 1960s-1970s (Gaddis 1964, Berlitz 1974). The Devil's Sea legend was popularized by Berlitz in 1974. Agonic line positions have been mapped since the 17th century. The dome model adopted these pre-existing cultural narratives and geomagnetic facts as a 'prediction' in v50.4 (2026-03-09). This is pure retrodiction of well-known phenomena.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No derivation from dome geometry exists. The dome site provides no formula connecting disc_radius, H(r), or any dome parameter to the positions of Bermuda or Japan. The 'geometric symmetry' is simply asserted, not derived. There is no calculation showing why dome geometry would produce anomalies at these specific longitudes.

holes_found.description: Cycle 1 recommended adding the specific 208° vs 180° longitude calculation (Bermuda ~71°W, Devil's Sea ~137°E = 208° separation, 28° off from claimed 180°). The current text now addresses Part (2) of the dome's claim but only in general terms ('does not correspond to any pattern in IGRF or CHAOS-7'). The specific coordinate arithmetic would be a much stronger, more falsifiable rebuttal that a defender cannot hand-wave away. Without the numbers, a defender can say 'approximately 180°' and move on.
holes_found.recommendation: Add to detail_evidence: 'The Bermuda region (centered ~25°N, 71°W) and Devil's Sea (~25°N, 137°E) are separated by 208° of longitude — 28° off the claimed 180° symmetry, a 15.6% deviation that exceeds the model's own tolerance thresholds for other WINs.'

holes_found.description: Our current code_analysis tags in wins.json show monitoring='hardcoded' for WIN-028, but the Cycle 1 review correctly identified that WIN-028 has NO monitoring code at all — it doesn't appear in any domain. The correct tag should be 'none', not 'hardcoded'. 'Hardcoded' implies there's at least a static pred==obs check in the code; 'none' means the WIN is completely absent from the monitoring engine. This is a tag error that affects Part 4.6 statistics.
holes_found.recommendation: Change code_analysis.monitoring from 'hardcoded' to 'none' in wins.json for WIN-028. This is a factual correction — there is no monitoring code for this WIN at all.

holes_found.description: The NOAA link (https://www.noaa.gov/) is still generic — it links to NOAA's homepage, not to any specific NOAA statement about the Bermuda Triangle. This was flagged in Cycle 1 and not fixed. A defender could note we're citing the homepage rather than a specific source, which looks lazy.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace with NOAA's specific page: https://oceanservice.noaa.gov/facts/bermudatri.html — or at minimum cite the specific NOAA statement that losses are attributable to 'environmental factors such as hurricanes, sudden weather shifts from the Gulf Stream, and hazardous shallow waters.'

holes_found.description: The rebuttal doesn't mention that the agonic lines themselves don't pass through either region. The western agonic line (~80°W) grazes only the western edge of the Bermuda Triangle, and the eastern agonic line (~100-120°E) is 17-37° west of the Devil's Sea at 137°E. This is a concrete factual point that strengthens the rebuttal.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a brief note: 'Neither agonic line actually passes through its associated region — the western agonic (~80°W) only grazes the Triangle's edge, while the eastern agonic (~100-120°E) lies well west of the Devil's Sea at 137°E.'

stronger_arguments.description: The dome added WIN-028 in v50.4 (2026-03-09) alongside WIN-027 and new predictions. This version history shows it was added as scorecard padding — there's no derivation, no formula, no monitoring. It's the weakest type of WIN: a pure cultural assertion with zero computational backing, adopted to inflate the win count from 26 to 29.

Agonic lines migrate significantly over decades due to secular variation of Earth's core field. The western agonic line passed through the Bermuda area in the 1700s but has since moved westward. Any 'symmetry' is ephemeral and fully explained by geodynamo theory. A fixed dome cavity cannot explain time-varying agonic positions — this is actually evidence AGAINST dome geometry.
sources: Mandea & Korte (2011), Geomagnetic Observations and Models, Springer

kernel_of_truth.description: Agonic lines are real and scientifically important geomagnetic features. There are indeed two primary agonic lines that run through roughly opposite hemispheres, and they had genuine navigational significance historically — compass bearings matched true bearings along them. The dome model correctly identifies these as noteworthy features of Earth's magnetic field.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Agonic lines are fully explained by the standard geodynamo model. They are not fixed geometric features of any cavity — they migrate over decades, which a static dome cannot explain. The claimed connection to 'disappearance zones' is debunked folklore, and the claimed 180° symmetry is actually 208° (15.6% off). The dome provides no derivation connecting its geometry to agonic line positions.

deeper_analysis:
Cycle 2 re-review of WIN-028 confirms the verdict holds as 'Refuted by Data.' The Cycle 1 moderate hole (not addressing the geometric symmetry claim) was PARTIALLY fixed — the detail_evidence now includes a Part (2) discussing how the dome conflates geographic folklore with geophysics, correctly notes the SAA is off Brazil not Bermuda, and references IGRF/CHAOS-7 models. However, the specific coordinate arithmetic (208° vs 180°) was not added, and the agonic line positions relative to the regions are not mentioned. These are the remaining opportunities to make the rebuttal bulletproof.

A NEW issue found in Cycle 2: the code_analysis.monitoring tag in wins.json is 'hardcoded' but should be 'none'. The Cycle 1 review's own dome_code_analysis clearly states 'WIN-028 is completely absent from monitor.py' and 'No data is fetched for this WIN.' The tag was set to 'hardcoded' but there is no hardcoded check either — there is literally no code. This affects the Part 4.6 statistics and should be corrected.

The minor issues (generic NOAA link, missing agonic line position details) from Cycle 1 remain unfixed. These aren't critical but represent missed opportunities for a tighter argument.

Overall, the verdict is solid — both halves of the dome's claim fail (disappearances debunked, symmetry geometry is wrong), the claim has zero computational backing in the dome's codebase, and it was added as scorecard padding in v50.4. The recommended action remains minor_edit to add the specific numbers.
